raw_request: Create PRD for Prescription Data Capture: Automated Intake of Prescription Reports from Email
primary_goal: Automate the intake of prescription data from emailed PDF reports into a governed database, eliminating manual handling and establishing a complete audit trail that ensures every report is captured, deduplicated, and traceable.
user_pain_point: Manual extraction of prescription data from PDF attachments does not scale, is error-prone due to manual transcription, and provides no audit trail. The business needs a reliable, verifiable, automated process that frees staff for higher-value work.

in_scope: Automated retrieval of emails from the designated business mailbox
Reading and parsing PDF attachments to extract prescription data
Capturing 23 fields of prescription information per record
Storing captured records in the organization's governed database
One-time historical load from a business-defined start date
Ongoing daily scheduled operation picking up only new emails since last run
Deduplication: preventing duplicate records when same prescription seen multiple times
Record updates when newer version of same prescription is received
Comprehensive audit logging: run records and email-level processing records
No human inbox interaction required (no forwarding, no folder movement)
out_of_scope: Reports arriving by channels other than the designated mailbox (paper, fax, other inboxes)
Downstream reporting, dashboards, or analytics built on the captured data
Changes to how prescription reports are produced or formatted by upstream sources
Replacement of existing pharmacy management systems
Changes to legacy spreadsheet data handling
ambiguous: PDF format: BRD states 'minor variations are tolerated' but does not define tolerance level or what constitutes 'wholesale format change'
Technology stack: BRD deliberately avoids prescribing implementation details (Section 15)
Specific 'governed database environment' definition not specified; only that it must be 'organization's governed database'
Exact daily run time not locked in: BRD says 'end of business day' but no specific hour or timezone defined
Historical load cutoff date: described as 'business-defined' but not yet agreed (Section 5.1 and 12.1)
Credential rotation cycle: referenced as 'organization's standard rotation cycle' but exact period not specified (Section 9.2)
Definition of 'Partial' run outcome conditions (Section 12.2): when does partial occur vs full failure
Email identification mechanism: how duplicates are recognized; is it message ID, sender+date+subject hash, or other?
Handling of format edge cases: what happens when PDF is unreadable, garbled, or partially corrupted?

affected_areas: Email intake and mailbox connection layer
PDF parsing and prescription data extraction engine
Prescription data validation and transformation logic
Audit logging and run tracking system

may_enable: Downstream reporting and dashboards on prescription data
Financial and revenue analysis (340B program tracking, volume reporting)
Audit trail reviews and internal control validation

ambiguities: Technology stack: BRD Section 15 explicitly states implementation details are not prescribed; email retrieval, PDF parsing, and database platform are open choices
PDF format tolerance: Section 9.1 assumes 'minor variations tolerated' but does not quantify tolerance or define escalation path for format changes
Cutoff date for historical load: Section 5.1 says 'business-defined start date' but no date is specified; must be negotiated at planning
Specific daily run time: Section 12.1 says 'end of business day' but no specific hour, timezone, or SLA window defined
Credential rotation period: Section 9.2 references 'organization's standard rotation cycle' [assumed to be 90 days based on typical corporate standards, but not stated in BRD]
Mailbox duplication detection: How does system identify if same email was processed before? (Message ID, content hash, sender+date+subject signature?)
Partial run definition: Section 12.2 defines three outcomes (Success, Partial, Failed) but thresholds for Partial vs Failed are not specified
PDF corruption handling: What is the fallback behavior if PDF cannot be parsed? (Email recorded as failed, or partial with extracted fields?)

Record update semantics: When prescription is seen again with updated values, Section 7.3 says record is updated, but 'update' operation is not detailed (overwrite all fields, selective merge, timestamp tracking?)

links_to_requirements: Section 2: Business Context and Problem Statement — establishes why manual process fails
Section 3.1: Primary Objectives — four key goals that define success
Section 5: Scope — clear boundaries of in and out
Section 7: Functional Capabilities — seven specific business functions to deliver
Section 8: Non-Functional Requirements — eight quality attributes expected
Section 9: Assumptions, Constraints, Dependencies — preconditions and limitations
Section 11: Data Captured Per Prescription — exact 23 fields to be extracted
Section 13: Acceptance Criteria — eight demonstrations of successful delivery
traceability.addresses_expected_outcome: Complete automation of prescription data intake with verifiable audit trail, deduplication safeguards, and recoverability to ensure data integrity, eliminate manual processing, scale with volume, and provide compliance-grade traceability.
